Title: Protection of naringenin chalcone by a pathogenesis-related 10 protein promotes flavonoid biosynthesis in Marchantia polymorpha

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Pathogenesis-related 10 (PR10) protein; Bet v 1/Major latex protein domain (PF00407); glycine-rich loop 4
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Tak-1 background; WT accessions Sey-1 (male) and Aud-2 (female) used in study
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: specialized pigments/phenolics associated with stress responses; auronidin gives red thallus pigmentation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of auronidins and flavones by protecting the shared unstable intermediate naringenin chalcone; loss of function reduces auronidin and flavone content.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR/Cas9 loss-of-function Mppr10.5 mutants showed strongly reduced red pigmentation, ~84% lower auronidin and ~60% lower flavone content under nutrient deficiency stress. 2. Recombinant MpPR10.5 bound naringenin chalcone with high affinity (Kd ~1.3 μM), stronger than to downstream flavonoids or bibenzyls. 3. MpPR10.5 prevented spontaneous self-cyclisation of naringenin chalcone to naringenin in vitro. 4. Loss of MpPR10.5 downregulated multiple phenylpropanoid/flavonoid genes and MpMYB14, consistent with metabolite-mediated feedback.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; transgenic overexpression; LC-MS/HPLC metabolite profiling; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; recombinant protein ligand-binding assay; in vitro stabilization/self-cyclisation assays
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Predicted cytosolic/nuclear localization not explicitly resolved in main text; NA
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly associated with auronidin production; induced under nutrient deficiency stress; correlated with MpMYB14 activity
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in nutrient deficiency stress conditions associated with auronidin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: naringenin chalcone; proposed functional association with MpCHS; MpCHIL; MpCHI; MpAUS
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nutrient deficiency stress induces auronidin and flavone biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S constitutive promoter for overexpression lines
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression did not significantly increase auronidins and only slightly increased flavones, indicating limited gain by single-gene overexpression; possible redundancy with other PR10 family members
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate metabolic engineering target to stabilize unstable flavonoid intermediates and improve flavonoid flux; likely best used with pathway/regulatory partners rather than alone due to limited overexpression effect and family redundancy
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified here as Common Gene because it is a nonenzymatic metabolite-binding/stabilizing protein rather than catalytic pathway enzyme or TF regulator. Paper proposes role in metabolite channeling/protection of naringenin chalcone and indirect feedback effects on pathway transcription.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as an auronidin regulator and positively associates with MpPR10.5 expression, but in this paper serves mainly as a downstream/background regulatory marker rather than the primary manipulated subject.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MpPR10.5 is described as a target of the auronidin regulator MpMYB14. 2. MpMYB14 transcript abundance was significantly downregulated in Mppr10.5 mutants under nutrient stress. 3. MpPR10.5 overexpression did not significantly affect MpMYB14 expression.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression analysis in Mppr10.5 and overexpression lines; prior literature cited for regulatory role
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates auronidin-associated genes including MpPR10.5 (based on paper statement that MpPR10.5 is a target of MpMYB14)
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Known auronidin regulator, but not experimentally dissected as a focal core gene in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Associated with nutrient stress-induced auronidin production
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in Mppr10.5 mutants under nutrient stress
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible indirect involvement in feedback responses inferred from Mppr10.5 mutant transcript changes; not directly tested
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulator for auronidin engineering, but not the experimental focus here
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included with caution: MpMYB14 is biologically important, but not a core experimentally manipulated gene in the Results of this paper.
